sections.html:
Early Web · 1994–1999

90s Web Revival

90s Web Revival reconstructs the raw, exuberant visual language of the early commercial internet — tiled background textures repeating across every pixel, beveled push-buttons straight out of Windows 95, pixel-font headings, and that unmistakable sense that someone had just discovered the blink tag and was absolutely going to use it. Born from real technical constraints — 8-bit monitors, 28.8k modems, and a 216-color web-safe palette — this aesthetic transforms what was once necessity into deliberate, knowing design.

You'll find it on personal developer sites built as affectionate homages to GeoCities-era homepages, on net art and digital humanities projects recreating early web culture as a living exhibit, and on alternative music and zine publisher sites where the late-90s internet aesthetic signals anti-corporate credibility. It also appears on novelty landing pages, throwback product campaigns, and any creative project that wants to say: we were online before it was cool.
